constraints.text: Laboratory personnel handling primary solutions should wear personal protective equipment including a toxic gas respirator mask fitted with charcoal filters approved by the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH)/Mine Safety Health Administration (MSHA) to prevent the inhalation of airborne particulates if not working in an approved hood or glove box.
Treat each chemical as a potential health hazard and minimize exposure to these chemicals.

steps.text: Recover samples in amber glass bottles, 500- or 1000-milliliters (mL) with leak-free PTFE-lined caps.
Use 125- and 250-mL amber glass bottles with PTFE-lined caps.
Use glass test tubes or small (e.g., 5 to 10 mL) amber vials.
Use 200-mL capacity thimble holder capable of holding 43 × 123-millimeter (mm) extraction thimbles, with receiving flask (typically round-bottom).
Use Dean-Stark or Barret with fluoropolymer stopcock trap to fit between the Soxhlet extractor body and the condenser.
Use quartz, glass, or glass fiber thimble, typically 43 × 123 mm to fit Soxhlet apparatus.
Use a hemispherical shaped heating mantle to fit round-bottom flask.

Use an apparatus consisting of a three-ball Snyder column, a flask with leak-free joint to accept the three-ball Snyder column at the top, a leak-free joint to receive a graduated concentration tube at the bottom and a heating mantle.
Use a nitrogen evaporative concentrator equipped with a water bath with the temperature controlled in the range of 30 to 60 °C (86 to 140 °F).
Use glass or PTFE 2-liter separatory funnels.
Use disposable pipettes, or glass serological pipettes typically 150 mm long × 6 mm ID.
Use liquid chromatography columns 200 to 300 mm long × 20 mm ID with 250-mL reservoir.
Use a gas chromatograph consisting of the following components.

Use an oven capable of maintaining the separation column at the proper operating temperature ± 1.0 °C (1.8 °F) and performing programmed increases in temperature at rates of at least 40 °C/min with isothermal hold.
Conduct a QC check on the cleaned adsorbent lot or batch following the extraction and analyses procedures in Section 11 of this method, including adding applicable labeled standards.
Store adsorbent in a solvent-rinsed nonporous clean container and secure lid.
Clean the glass wool to meet the specifications in Section 13.1 of this method.
Use deionized or distilled water meeting requirements in Section 13.1 of this method and store in its original container or in a clean glass container with a PTFE-lined screw cap.
If previously used, dry silica gel at 175 °C (347 °F) for two hours.
Use new silica gel as received or other types of desiccants (equivalent or better), subject to the approval of the Administrator.
Use potassium hydroxide, American Chemical Society (ACS) grade, 2% (weight/volume) in water.
Evaluate sodium sulfate for cleanliness prior to use with an LMB.
Store sodium sulfate in a clean glass container with a PTFE-lined screw cap.
Weigh 40 g of sodium hydroxide into a 1-liter volumetric flask and dilute to 1 liter with water.
Use high-boiling alkanes as keeper solvents, ensuring not to take samples to dryness when using lower alkane homologs.
Use column chromatography packing materials as needed to prepare sample extracts by fractionation and removal of interferences.
Tune the HRMS instrument to a resolving power of at least 10,000 at 10% percent of the peak height or 25,000 at 50% percent of the peak height.
Assess the resolution at three exact m/z’s representing the low-, mid-, and high-m/z range of the masses used to measure the target compound class.
Calibrate the HRGC/HRMS system using a minimum of five concentrations over a range that brackets expected field sample concentrations and the concentration of isotopically labeled samples.

preconditions.text: GC column or column systems used to measure PCDD/PCDF must meet the column separation requirements in Section 6.5.2.1 of this method and the applicable requirements in Sections 10.2.3.5 and 11.4.3.4 of this method using the continuing calibration verification.

GC column or column systems used to measure PAH must meet the column separation requirements in Section 6.5.2.2 of this method and the applicable requirements in Sections 10.2.3.5 and 11.4.3.4 of this method using the continuing calibration check.
GC column or column systems used to measure PCB must meet the column separation requirements in Section 6.5.2.3 of this method and the applicable requirements.

constraints.text: Samples containing hydrogen chloride or sulfuric acid to pH <2 are hazardous and must be handled and disposed in accordance with federal, state, and local regulations.
